title: A Fish Called Selma

jokes.text: Well, it's not quite a mop and it's not quite a puppet, but, man- So, to answer your question, I don't know.
jokes.setup: Lisa asks what a Muppet is, setting up expectation for Homer to provide a helpful explanation
jokes.punchline: Homer's nonsensical partial explanation followed by complete admission of ignorance
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer - starts with confidence but trails off into admitting complete ignorance. Perfect character integration and efficient structure.

jokes.text: Why'd they make that one Muppet out of leather?
jokes.setup: Family watching Troy McClure on TV with actual Muppets
jokes.punchline: Lisa mistakes Troy McClure for a 'leather Muppet'
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective visual joke about Troy's appearance being puppet-like.

jokes.text: Those weird things they say he does down at the aquarium. Why, I heard- Oh, Homer! That's just an urban legend. People don't do that type of thing with fish.
jokes.setup: Homer mentions Troy's bizarre personal life and aquarium rumors
jokes.punchline: Marge's innocent denial of fish-related activities sets up the episode's running gag
jokes.explanation: Establishes the fish fetish subplot with perfect innocent/knowing dynamic between Marge and Homer.

jokes.text: Troy McClure's a perfect gentleman, like Bing Crosby or J.F.K.
jokes.setup: Marge defending Troy's character
jokes.punchline: Compares him to two men with notorious personal scandals
jokes.explanation: Brilliant ironic comparison - both Crosby and JFK had well-known scandals. Marge's innocence makes it funnier.

jokes.text: You may remember me from such films as The Greatest Story Ever Hulaed and They Came to Burgle Carnegie Hall.
jokes.setup: Troy's signature self-introduction format
jokes.punchline: Absurd fake movie titles with wordplay ('Hulaed' and burglary pun)
jokes.explanation: Perfect Troy McClure moment - the signature catchphrase with cleverly absurd fake movies. Character-defining.

jokes.text: You wouldn't ask a handsome man like me to wear glasses! It'd be a crime against nature!
jokes.setup: DMV worker tells Troy he needs glasses
jokes.punchline: Troy's dramatic overreaction treating glasses as a crime against nature
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Troy's vanity and dramatic tendencies.

jokes.text: Well, they do kind of make you look like a nerd. Tell you what. Just go down to the D.M.V. tomorrow and try to pass that eye test. I'll tear up this ticket, but I'm still gonna have to ask you for a bribe.
jokes.setup: Officer seeming to help Troy with glasses issue
jokes.punchline: Casual corruption - still asks for bribe after being helpful
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from helpful to corrupt in matter-of-fact way.

jokes.text: How come no Chippendales dancers ever come in to renew their licenses? They carpool. That's the problem.
jokes.setup: Selma wondering about attractive men at DMV
jokes.punchline: Patty's mundane explanation for lack of attractive customers
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for the twins' different perspectives and Patty's deadpan logic.

jokes.text: No. What I said was he sleeps with the fishes. You see- Uh, Tony, please! No. I just ate a whole plate of 'dingamagoo.'
jokes.setup: Tony misunderstanding 'sleeps with the fishes' as death threat
jokes.punchline: Tony's stomach upset from mysterious Italian food prevents mob explanation
jokes.explanation: Mild misdirection joke playing with mob movie tropes and fish rumors.

jokes.text: I remember you from such films as Meet Joe Blow and Give My Remains to Broadway.
jokes.setup: Selma recognizing Troy with his catchphrase format
jokes.punchline: More absurd fake movie titles with puns on real movies
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Troy's signature line with new fake movies. Shows Selma buying into his celebrity.

jokes.text: W... seven, star, pound.
jokes.setup: Troy trying to read eye chart without glasses
jokes.punchline: He reads symbols instead of letters, showing how bad his vision is
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective visual gag showing Troy's vanity overriding basic vision needs.

jokes.text: Look, Miss... 'Boo-Veer.'
jokes.setup: Troy trying to read Selma's name tag
jokes.punchline: Mispronounces 'Bouvier' as 'Boo-Veer' due to poor vision
jokes.explanation: Continues the vision joke while showing Troy's attempt at charm despite inability to see.

jokes.text: Hmm? Hmm. Hmm. Mm-mmm. Hmm. Hmm. Oh.!
jokes.setup: Selma looking for Troy's photo on restaurant's celebrity wall
jokes.punchline: Series of disappointed reactions as she searches unsuccessfully
jokes.explanation: Visual comedy showing Selma's dawning realization that Troy isn't as famous as he claims.

jokes.text: Well, I think I'm getting Repetitive Stress Disorder from scratching my butt all day.
jokes.setup: Troy asking about her interesting DMV job
jokes.punchline: Selma's TMI response about scratching herself all day
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selma moment - oversharing disgusting personal details on a date. Great character consistency.

jokes.text: I've been reading a lot of scripts lately. You know, it's a lot cheaper than going to the movies.
jokes.setup: Selma asking what movie projects he's working on
jokes.punchline: Troy admits he reads scripts because he can't afford movies
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment showing Troy's fallen status while maintaining his spin-doctor mentality.

jokes.text: Here you go, boys. A little something for page one.
jokes.setup: Paparazzi taking photos of Troy with Selma
jokes.punchline: Troy treating mundane date as front-page news material
jokes.explanation: Shows Troy's delusional self-importance and desperate hunger for attention.

jokes.text: Maybe those rumors about his fish fetish weren't true after all. This changes everything. I'd pay to see him in a movie now. If only that were possible.
jokes.setup: Homer seeing Troy with a woman in the newspaper
jokes.punchline: Homer's logic that dating a human disproves fish rumors, ending with dig at Troy's lack of current films
jokes.explanation: Good callback to fish rumors with Homer's flawed logic and final burn about Troy's career.

jokes.text: You haven't found me work in 12 years. Oh, you! Jury duty is work.
jokes.setup: Troy complaining his agent hasn't found him work in 12 years
jokes.punchline: Agent's dismissive response that jury duty counts as work
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures the sleazy agent dynamic and Troy's desperation. Great economy of words.

jokes.text: You may remember me from such dates as last night's dinner.
jokes.setup: Troy calling Selma using his signature introduction format
jokes.punchline: Applies the movie catchphrase to their personal date
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback that perfectly adapts Troy's signature line to romantic context. Shows his inability to be genuine even in personal moments.

jokes.text: Well, not much happens to me... but I once had dinner with a movie star... and it was the most wonderful night of my life. Really? Who was it? George Segal? I hear he plays the banjo.
jokes.setup: Selma telling story about dining with a movie star (Troy)
jokes.punchline: Troy doesn't realize she's talking about him, guesses random has-been actor
jokes.explanation: Great dramatic irony showing Troy's ego blindness and Selma's genuine appreciation for their date.

jokes.text: Excuse me. I ordered a Zima, not 'emphysema.' Please, don't smoke in our restaurant. We don't serve contemporary California cuisine in your lungs.
jokes.setup: Waiter objecting to smoking in restaurant
jokes.punchline: Pretentious wordplay comparing Zima/emphysema and bizarre cuisine metaphor
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay and satire of pretentious restaurant culture, though a bit wordy.

jokes.text: You know, smoke actually smells good when it's coming outta you.
jokes.setup: Troy and Selma bonding over smoking
jokes.punchline: Troy's awkward attempt at romantic compliment about her smoke
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing Troy's inability to be romantic and Selma's low standards.

jokes.text: My God! It's like five cigarettes at once! Oh, my head is swimming! That's not cigars, baby. That's love.
jokes.setup: Selma overwhelmed by cigar smoke
jokes.punchline: Troy misattributing her physical reaction to romance
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection joke showing Troy's complete lack of romantic insight.

jokes.text: We may remember- Woman? Huh. Okay. We may remember Troy from such films as The Verdict Was Mail Fraud and Leper in the Backfield.
jokes.setup: Reporter describing Troy's romance
jokes.punchline: Reporter's confused pause at calling Selma a 'woman' plus more fake movie titles
jokes.explanation: Callback to fake movies gag plus subtle dig at Selma's attractiveness through reporter's hesitation.

jokes.text: Looks like you were wrong when you called him a washed-up deviant, Laurie. All right. Make sure my iguana's okay.
jokes.setup: News anchor commenting on Troy's comeback
jokes.punchline: Abrupt shift to concern about iguana
jokes.explanation: Random non-sequitur that's mildly amusing but doesn't land strongly. Typical Simpsons background weirdness.

jokes.text: Garbage collection is Monday. If you want to throw out a box, you have to cut it up.
jokes.setup: Troy showing Selma around his modern home
jokes.punchline: Mundane garbage rules presented as house tour highlights
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Troy treating mundane details as important information.

jokes.text: I'll be sleeping downstairs in the visitor's center. I'll see you in the morning, and get ready for tennis. It comes on at 10:00.
jokes.setup: Troy explaining sleeping arrangements to new wife
jokes.punchline: Reveals they'll watch tennis on TV, not play it
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing Troy's sedentary lifestyle and separate bedrooms revealing marriage problems.

jokes.text: Uh, the movie or the planet?
jokes.setup: Agent asking if he's heard of Planet of the Apes
jokes.punchline: Troy's confused question about whether it's the movie or actual planet
jokes.explanation: Perfect Troy moment showing his disconnect from reality and tendency to take things too literally.

jokes.text: Get your paws off me, you dirty ape! He can talk! He can talk. He can talk. He can talk! He can talk! He can talk.! He can talk.!
jokes.setup: Troy delivering famous Planet of the Apes line in musical
jokes.punchline: Ensemble's repetitive chanting of 'He can talk!' escalates absurdly
jokes.explanation: Good escalation gag satirizing musical theater's tendency toward repetitive choruses.

jokes.text: Dr. Zaius, Dr. Zaius Oh, Dr. Zaius Dr. Zaius, Dr. Zaius What's wrong with me I think you're crazy Want a second opinion You're also lazy
jokes.setup: Musical number based on Planet of the Apes
jokes.punchline: Medical consultation turns into insulting diagnosis song
jokes.explanation: Highly quotable musical parody that became a cultural reference. Creative adaptation of source material.

jokes.text: Can I play the piano anymore Of course you can Well, I couldn't before
jokes.setup: Character asking if he can play piano after treatment
jokes.punchline: Classic vaudeville joke - he couldn't play before either
jokes.explanation: Classic old vaudeville joke with perfect structure, though not original. Works well in context.

jokes.text: I hate every ape I see From chimpan-A to chimpan-Z
jokes.setup: Continuing the musical number about hating apes
jokes.punchline: Wordplay combining 'chimpanzee' with A to Z format
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that maintains the musical's absurd tone while showing creativity in the pun.

jokes.text: Hi. I remember you from such filmstrips as Locker Room Towel Fight: The Blinding of Larry Driscoll. I was one of the first to speak out against horseplay.
jokes.setup: Troy introducing himself to kids using his signature format
jokes.punchline: Absurd educational filmstrip title with over-serious anti-horseplay stance
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that adapts Troy's signature to educational context with perfectly absurd title and self-important follow-up.

jokes.text: I know. I know. It's Selma, right?
jokes.setup: Lisa asking Homer to guess which sister found love
jokes.punchline: Homer's confident but delayed obvious answer
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer moment - acting like he's clever for stating the obvious.

jokes.text: Yeah, that's a good idea, Homer... but they've already made some movies about World War II. Aw, hell! Well, what about Dracula?
jokes.setup: Troy dismissing Homer's WWII movie idea as already done
jokes.punchline: Homer's dejection followed by suggesting equally overdone Dracula movies
jokes.explanation: Good character interaction showing Homer's obliviousness to overused concepts.

jokes.text: And do you, Selma Bouvier Terwilliger Bouvier... take the fabulous Troy McClure to be your lawful wedded husband? I already told you yes.
jokes.setup: Minister reading Selma's multiple married names at wedding
jokes.punchline: Selma's impatient response showing this isn't her first wedding
jokes.explanation: Good character detail showing Selma's multiple marriages and impatience with ceremony.

jokes.text: And do you, Selma Bouvier Terwilliger Bouvier... take the fabulous Troy McClure to be your lawful wedded husband?
jokes.setup: Wedding ceremony
jokes.punchline: Selma's multiple last names and Troy being called 'fabulous'
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Selma's marriage history and Troy's self-importance. Solid character-based humor.

jokes.text: Mmm. Pointy.
jokes.setup: Homer eating wedding cake bride and groom figures
jokes.punchline: Homer's matter-of-fact reaction to eating plastic decorations
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - eating inedible objects and casually commenting on texture. Great economy.

jokes.text: Apparently, he doesn't really love Selma... and the marriage is just a sham to help his career. Well, enough talk. Let's snuggle.
jokes.setup: Homer revealing Troy's marriage is fake
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately shifting to romance after delivering devastating news
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Homer's inability to read emotional situations and poor timing.

jokes.text: I want to see the Troy McClure I remember from such films... as Make-Out King of Montana and The Electric Gigolo.
jokes.setup: Selma trying to seduce Troy on their wedding night
jokes.punchline: Uses his signature catchphrase format to reference fake romantic movies
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Selma trying to use Troy's language while creating uncomfortable seduction scene.

jokes.text: Paramount wants you for a buddy comedy with Rob Lowe and Hugh Grant. Those sick freaks?
jokes.setup: Agent offering Troy a movie with two handsome actors
jokes.punchline: Troy calling them sick freaks, implying his own issues are different
jokes.explanation: Great irony - Troy judging other actors when he has his own scandalous reputation.

jokes.text: My sisters have come down with a case of the green-eyed gazungas.
jokes.setup: Selma responding to her sisters' warnings about Troy
jokes.punchline: Made-up phrase combining jealousy with nonsense word 'gazungas'
jokes.explanation: Good character moment with creative wordplay, though 'gazungas' is a bit forced.

jokes.text: You're tied down to a man who'd have to bathe to be a slob.
jokes.setup: Selma insulting Marge's life situation
jokes.punchline: Escalating insult suggesting Homer is below slob-level cleanliness
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating insult with great economy and memorable phrasing. Perfect character voice for angry Selma.

jokes.text: Sure, baby. Is that a problemo?
jokes.setup: Selma asking if their marriage is a sham
jokes.punchline: Troy's casual confirmation with made-up slang word 'problemo'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Troy moment - casual about devastating revelation while using silly made-up slang.

jokes.text: Sure, I do, like I love Fresca. Isn't that enough?
jokes.setup: Selma asking if Troy loves her
jokes.punchline: Troy comparing his love for her to a soft drink brand
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly casual devastating comparison showing Troy's complete emotional disconnect. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: Are you gay? Gay? I wish! If I were gay, there'd be no problem.
jokes.setup: Selma asking about Troy's sexual orientation
jokes.punchline: Troy wishing he were gay because it would be easier than his actual issues
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy escalation that deepens the fish fetish mystery while being genuinely funny and surprising.

jokes.text: Just smile for the cameras and enjoy Mr. Troy's Wild Ride.
jokes.setup: Troy trying to convince Selma to stay in fake marriage
jokes.punchline: Refers to their relationship using amusement park ride terminology
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Troy's showbiz mentality and treating relationships like entertainment.

jokes.text: You can't use Montana, Dakota or Florida- they're taken. Mmm. Oregon? Oh! Pacific Northwest. Very hot.
jokes.setup: Agent discussing baby names as career move
jokes.punchline: Treating state names like show business trends with 'Pacific Northwest' being hot
jokes.explanation: Good satire of Hollywood agent mentality treating everything as marketing opportunity.

jokes.text: Think what it'll mean! Not just the McBain movie, but maybe my own fragrance- 'Smellin' of Troy'!
jokes.setup: Troy excited about career opportunities from having a baby
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous fragrance name with intentionally bad grammar
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Troy's delusional career fantasies and poor product naming skills.

jokes.text: From now on, she's smoking for two!
jokes.setup: Troy offering Selma cigarettes when trying to conceive
jokes.punchline: Twisted version of 'eating for two' pregnancy phrase applied to smoking
jokes.explanation: Dark but clever subversion of pregnancy cliché that shows both characters' unhealthy habits.

jokes.text: Remember how 'alive with pleasure' they said we were?
jokes.setup: Troy referencing their Newport cigarette ad to explain their relationship
jokes.punchline: Treating advertising copy as genuine emotional memory
jokes.explanation: Perfect Troy moment - confusing advertising work with real relationships, showing his disconnect from genuine emotion.

jokes.text: Oh, great! We'll adopt. I'll call my agent. He'll find some kid who wants in on the deal.
jokes.setup: Selma refusing to have a biological child
jokes.punchline: Troy treating adoption like a business transaction
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about Troy's complete inability to understand normal human relationships. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: Come on, JubJub. Let's go home, and I'll microwave you some nice roaches.
jokes.setup: Selma leaving Troy and addressing her pet iguana
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of microwaving roaches as pet food
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Selma's matter-of-fact approach to disgusting pet care.

jokes.text: The Contrabulous Fabtraption of Professor Horatio Hufnagel
jokes.setup: Reporter describing Troy's new independent film project
jokes.punchline: Completely nonsensical title with made-up words
jokes.explanation: Highly quotable nonsense title that perfectly captures Troy's pretentious artistic ambitions with made-up words.

tmdb_overview: Washed up and plagued by scandal, Troy McClure sees a marriage to Selma as his ticket back to success.
insight: Troy McClure vehicle delivers 54 jokes at steady 2.6-per-minute clip, anchored by character comedy.
